demographics.living_situation: lives with parent; parent is a primary carer

opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: Her hands are locked completely shut in pain, she says her lips feel dead, and she is terrified she is going to have a seizure. (call made by the patient's parent, who is with her)
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: It came on over the last few hours and in the last hour her hands cramped up and locked shut and she can't open them; she's panicking and breathing really fast.

symptom_narrative.onset: about 6 hours ago the numbness and tingling around her mouth started; about 1 hour ago her hands cramped up hard and locked shut
symptom_narrative.duration: 6 hours of tingling, 1 hour of the locked-hand cramps and getting worse
symptom_narrative.character: numb, dead feeling around the lips, then intense painful cramping that pulled her fingers and hands into a rigid bent position she can't undo
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: the hand pain is severe and she is extremely frightened
symptom_narrative.location_as_patient_describes: around the mouth and lips, and both hands/wrists
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: started slowly then got much worse in the last hour
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: it seems worse the more panicked and fast-breathing she gets
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: a gritty feeling in the skin over her joints; very anxious and breathing fast
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: her hands are locked so she can't use them; she is immobilised and highly distressed

past_medical_history.condition_as_patient_states: a genetic condition she was born with that affects how her body handles calcium; the parent calls it Albright's / pseudohypoparathyroidism
past_medical_history.how_managed: takes a calcium tablet and a special vitamin D tablet every day

past_medical_history.condition_as_patient_states: short stature and a rounder face that the family associates with her genetic condition

past_medical_history.how_managed: parent assists with day-to-day care and medications

past_medical_history.condition_as_patient_states: had cataracts in both eyes at a young age

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: lives with her parent who is a carer and made the call; parent reports she has been refusing her tablets for the past four days after a change in her behaviour

access_barriers.other: intellectual disability makes verbal reassurance and self-report difficult; history and cooperation are parent-mediated

objective_data_offered.value: both hands drawn up and locked into a rigid bent position; fingers cannot be straightened
breathing fast and shallow, visibly hyperventilating and very agitated
parent reports she is frightened, hyperventilating, and says the skin over her joints feels gritty
